NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE METEOROLOGIST WITNESSED A WATERSPOUT OVER THE COASTAL ATLANTIC WATERS JUST OFF MIAMI BEACH -- OBSERVED FROM THE VANTAGE POINT OF DOWNTOWN MIAMI ACROSS THE STREET FROM AMERICAN AIRLINES ARENA. WATERSPOUT WAS EVIDENT AS A DISTINCT, DARKENED COLUMN EXTENDING FROM A WALL CLOUD TO THE OCEAN SURFACE -- SEPARATE FROM ANY PRINCIPAL RAIN CORES. PRESENCE OF WALL CLOUD AND FUNNEL WERE APPARENT FROM A SEPARATE SOCIAL-MEDIA POST SHOWING A PICTURE TAKEN IN CLOSER PROXIMITY TO MIAMI BEACH. THIS WATERSPOUT ACCOMPANIED GENERATING CONVECTIVE CELLS WITHIN A REGIME OF MODEST ISENTROPIC ASCENT. DIFFERENTIAL VERTICAL MOTION ACCOMPANYING THESE UPDRAFTS RESULTED IN SURFACE-VORTICITY STRETCHING / AMPLIFICATION ALONG A NORTH-SOUTH COMPOSITE / AMALGAMATED LAND-BREEZE / OUTFLOW BOUNDARY THAT WAS SHIFTING WESTWARD OVER THE ATLANTIC WATERS OF SOUTH FLORIDA AND TOWARD/INTO BISCAYNE BAY -- AUGMENTED BY BAROCLINICALLY-GENERATED VORTICITY PERIPHERAL TO INCIPIENT WEAK CONVECTIVE COLD POOLS.
